description_long: This is a medieval-style helmet, likely inspired by a bascinet or sallet, featuring a pointed dome and a pronounced, downward-curving visor that covers the lower face. The helmet is constructed from dark, matte-finished metal, giving it a robust and protective appearance. Rivets are visible along the lower edge, securing the visor and possibly other internal components. The sides of the helmet extend downward to provide additional coverage for the cheeks and jaw, while the back flares out to protect the neck. There are no visible ventilation holes, decorative elements, or branding on the surface, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall form is smooth and rounded, with a single central ridge running from the forehead to the back.
description: A dark, pointed medieval-style metal helmet with a smooth, matte finish and a pronounced lower-face visor. The design is plain, robust, and protective, with no visible graphics or vents.
description_view_0: Front-top view showing the pointed dome, central ridge, and the open lower-face visor.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the rounded dome, extended cheek guard, and the flared neck protection.
description_view_2: Direct front view emphasizing the symmetrical pointed shape and the full coverage of the visor.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the smooth curvature, cheek guard, and the rear flare for neck protection.
